summary: Microsoft has launched PatternShare, a community resource that aggregates pattern summaries from various influential pattern authors. The platform aims to facilitate discussion and exploration of how different software patterns interconnect. Ward Cunningham, a foundational figure in the patterns community, led much of the development effort. The resource includes contributions from major pattern authors including Fowler, the Gang of Four, POSA authors, Hohpe/Woolf, Evans, and Microsoft.
key_points: PatternShare is a Microsoft-backed community platform for software patterns
The platform aggregates patterns from multiple authoritative sources to show interconnections
Ward Cunningham, with his deep pattern heritage, led the initiative
Contributors include the most influential names in software patterns literature
money_quotes: The idea is to bring together pattern summaries from many pattern authors and provide a platform for discussion and further exploration of the interconnections between them.
Much of the work was led by Ward Cunningham, whose pattern lineage is second to none.
key_insight: Aggregating patterns from multiple authoritative sources into a shared platform enables better understanding of how different patterns interconnect and relate to each other.
